$comment: c76: crosses BOTH c76 thresholds in one callee so it exercises the wide-call arg materialization AND the D-FF3 SysV mixed-class overflow together. int probe(int i1..i8, double d1..d11) — i7,i8 overflow the 6 SysV GPR arg regs; d9,d10,d11 overflow the 8 SysV FP arg regs. The pre-regalloc lowerWideCallArgs pass must split BOTH the int and FP overflow args to `store_outgoing_arg` carriers (D-AS-REGALLOC-WIDE-CALL-OPERAND-COUNT), and the callee reads the GPR-overflow and FP-overflow stack args from DISTINCT monotonic offsets (D-FF3). WITNESS: register args uniform (=1), overflow args distinct-valued and distinct-weighted so a wrong overflow-slot offset OR a GPR/FP slot collision changes the exit code. isum = 21 + (2*7+3*8)=38 -> 59; dsum = 36 + (2*9+3*10+4*11)=92 -> 128; total 187. FP args from a VOLATILE global (`one`==1) so the release arm does not trip D-OPT-RELEASE-FP-CONSTANT-UNLOADED-XMM (c77). RED-ON-DISABLE (two tiers): revert the wide-call pass -> error[L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc]; revert the D-FF3 offset fix -> SysV returns a wrong (not 187) value. => 187.
